TimeEntry.Title: FIRST Insurance Funding - Payment Invoice - 105158638
TimeEntry.Notes: Amy mailed in regarding the concern was related to emails coming from firstinsite@firstinsurancefunding.com, which were suspected to be spam or phishing attempts. Upon investigation, we verified that the domain firstinsurancefunding.com is legitimate and officially belongs to First Insurance Funding, a division of Wintrust Financial Corporation. The sender’s domain, website, and mail infrastructure (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) were all confirmed as valid and properly configured

TimeEntry.Title: New missed call from Elyse Backer Ext.355
TimeEntry.Notes: The user reported an issue where voicemail notifications and missed call emails were being received even when calls were answered. Upon investigation, it was found that the call forwarding time was set to only 1 second, which caused the system to trigger a voicemail and missed call notification almost immediately before the user could answer. To resolve the issue, the call forwarding time was increased to 10 seconds, allowing sufficient time for the user’s phone to ring before forwarding to voicemail. A test call was then made to verify the configuration, and the user confirmed that she no longer receives any missed call or voicemail notification emails after answering calls. The issue has been successfully resolved and verified.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: SPAM. Fwd: Mark Nichols sent a Payment Overview and Remittance File for Vintage group 09:40 AM
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Spam Email Report – Domain Blocked

Issue:
User Colleen Rozen (Vintage Group) reported receiving a spam email titled:

“Mark Nichols sent a Payment Overview and Remittance File for Vintage group 09:40 AM”
The message appeared to be a phishing attempt containing suspicious content.

Actions Taken:

  • Reviewed the reported email and confirmed it as phishing/spam.

  • Logged into the Microsoft 365 Defender Security Portal.

  • Blocked the sender’s email address and associated domain to prevent future delivery.

  • Verified the domain was successfully added to the Tenant Block List.

  • Notified the reporting user that the threat has been mitigated.

Result:
The sender and domain have been successfully blocked across the tenant. No further spam emails from this source will be delivered.

Status: ? Closed – Resolved

TimeEntry.Title: New missed call from Elyse Backer Ext.355
TimeEntry.Notes:
The user reported that they continued to receive missed call notifications even after answering calls.

  • Logged into the 3CX portal and reviewed both the Event Logs and Activity Logs for any anomalies.

  • Verified and adjusted relevant settings under General Options, ensuring "Ring my mobile simultaneously" was disabled.

  • Enabled the Rebound feature and attempted to test the issue by calling the user, but reached voicemail.

  • The user later returned the call and mentioned that calls were no longer forwarding directly to her mobile, and instead prompted options (1 to transfer, 2 for voicemail).

  • Logged back into the 3CX portal and disabled the Rebound feature.

  • Performed another test call — user confirmed that calls were now being directly routed to her mobile.

  • Updated the call forwarding rule to route calls to the external number and conducted a final test.

  • The user confirmed she no longer receives missed call alerts after answering.

  • Requested the user to check with a colleague to confirm everything is functioning correctly.

  • User verified with her colleague and confirmed the issue has been resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Email not going through

Ticket Summary:
User Amy Moore (Vintage Group) reported that an email with an attachment was not being received by the recipient mpelczar@ohanare.com, while all other emails were going through successfully.

Verified the mail flow and message trace in Microsoft 365 — all emails to the recipient were shown as delivered successfully from our end. Advised the user to upload the file to OneDrive, generate a sharing link, and send the link to the recipient instead, to confirm successful delivery.

Status: Verified and advised alternate method – ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Vintage Group - Email Storage
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue: Mailbox Storage Limit Reached — Jennifer Turriciano (Vintage Group)

Description:
User Jennifer Turriciano (it@vintagegrouppre.com) reported that her mailbox was nearly full, with only 399 MB free of 49.5 GB remaining. Despite deleting items and cleaning up folders, the mailbox continued to hit its storage threshold.
Request for mailbox storage increase was escalated by Colleen Rozen (Director of Operations) via email.

Actions Taken:

  1. Verified Mailbox Status:

    • Checked mailbox quota in Exchange Admin Center (EAC) under the user Jennifer Turriciano.

    • Confirmed primary mailbox nearing the 49.5 GB limit.

  2. Reviewed License Assignment:

    • Found user assigned to Microsoft 365 Business Standard, which includes a 50 GB mailbox cap.

  3. Upgraded License:

    • Upgraded the user’s license from Microsoft 365 Business Standard → Microsoft 365 Business Premium (Exchange Online Plan 2).

    • Plan 2 provides a 100 GB mailbox and auto-archiving capabilities.

  4. Configured Archiving:

    • Enabled Online Archive Mailbox for automatic archiving of older items.

    • Verified archiving policy applied successfully in Exchange Admin Center.

  5. Validated in Exchange Admin Center:

    • Confirmed new storage quota reflected:

      • Prohibit Send/Receive: 99 GB

      • Archive Mailbox: Enabled

    • Sent confirmation test email — successfully delivered.

  6. Notified Requestor:

    • Updated Colleen Rozen confirming the license upgrade and expanded mailbox capacity.

Outcome:

? Mailbox upgraded to 100 GB limit.
? Online Archive mailbox enabled.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Vintage Group - Alyssa Martinez
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue: Password Reset Request — Alyssa Martinez (Vintage Group)

Description:
Received a request from Colleen Rozen (Director of Operations) to reset Alyssa Martinez’s Microsoft 365 password and provide the new credentials securely. The request included a verified phone number for credential delivery.

Actions Taken:

  1. Verified Identity and Authorization:

    • Confirmed the request was initiated by an authorized contact (Colleen Rozen).

    • Verified the associated user account for Alyssa Martinez in Microsoft 365 Admin Center.

  2. Password Reset:

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Vintage Group - Jennifer Adksion
TimeEntry.Notes:
Troubleshot 3CX call-audio issue; identified faulty headset; connected working headset and verified 3CX softphone calls—issue resolved.

Detail (work log):

  • Reviewed Windows sound settings and 3CX > Settings > Audio Devices.

  • Performed 3CX echo test; microphone not detected from existing headset.

  • Isolated issue to endpoint hardware (headset) after verifying device drivers and default input/output devices.

  • Connected a working headset, set as default in Windows and selected in 3CX.

  • Retested internal and external calls; audio bidirectional and stable.

Resolution: Replaced/connected a working headset; updated default audio devices; confirmed successful test

TimeEntry.Title: Jennifer -3CX issue on softphone and on Cell phone
TimeEntry.Notes:
I received a call from Jennifer as she had an issue with her computer and the Softphone (3CX). I asked her for a remote session and checked the issue. It turned out to be a network issue and a headphone issue. The ringer was also off. I started by addressing the network issue, checked the firewall, and noticed pending updates for Wi-Fi. Then, I connected the second set of headphones, configured them in 3CX, and turned on the ringer. After a test call, everything worked fine.

However, for 3CX on her cell phone, it wasn’t working. I reinstalled 3CX on the phone and reconfigured it, but it still didn't work. I checked the 3CX events, but everything seemed normal. I then configured her account on my side to confirm the issue, but the phone still didn’t ring. I logged into 3CX Admin and enabled the push notifications, and after that, it started working. Everything is working fine now, so I can close the ticket

TimeEntry.Title: FW: SPAM FW: GMA's Deals and Steals
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a report indicating that a spam email appeared to have originated from Kelly Skotovsky and was spread internally. Upon investigation, it was confirmed that the spam message was sent only to Amy, and no similar messages were found in the mail flow logs for the past 10 days.
To ensure a thorough review, an enhanced summary report covering the last 50 days is being generated to trace the source of the message. This extended analysis will help determine where Kelly received the spam from and allow the support team to block the sender at the source level.
Status: 10-day summary review completed. 50-day report generation in progress for deeper investigation. Team will take necessary action to block the identified sender and update the user once completed.

TimeEntry.Title: FW: SPAM FW: GMA's Deals and Steals
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed and continued investigation of the spam message incident originally identified by Goutam. Downloaded and verified the message trace report, which confirmed that the email was sent internally and not from any external address. The message originated from Kelly’s mailbox and was successfully delivered to Kelly and Amy. Performed a detailed review of Kelly’s account, including mailbox configuration, forwarding rules, and delegation settings — no suspicious rules, external forwarding, or unauthorized access were found. Checked sign-in logs, which showed multiple failed login attempts from foreign locations (including Brussels, Belgium), indicating potential credential exposure. Communicated findings to Colleen and Amy, and recommended resetting Kelly’s password, re-registering MFA, and signing out of all active sessions. Upon approval, reset Kelly’s Microsoft 365 password, shared the new credentials securely with Colleen via text message, and initiated the MFA re-registration requirement. Verified that mailbox settings remain clean and that no additional suspicious activity is present. Account now secured and pending user confirmation of successful sign-in and MFA setup.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Can you please assist on this?
TimeEntry.Notes:
Investigated report that user Shawan Marie was unable to view the “Permissions for this document library” option within the SharePoint site Solstice 70 Leaves 11/30/2025.

Reviewed the document library’s permission structure and confirmed that the library is inheriting permissions from the parent site. Verified that Shawan Marie is not listed as an individual user nor a member of any SharePoint group (External Members, External Owners, External Visitors, etc.) that has the required access level.

Because the user does not have Edit or Full Control permissions, the system does not display administrative options such as:

Library Settings

Permissions for this document library

Manage access

Advanced settings

Confirmed this behavior is expected due to insufficient privileges.

Provided guidance on required permissions:

Add Shawan Marie to External Owners (Full Control), or

Break inheritance and grant her Full Control/Edit on this library only, depending on organizational needs.

Awaiting direction from customer on preferred permission level before applying changes.

TimeEntry.Title: WiFi is down at the office
TimeEntry.Notes: Found that internet was working for ethernet connected devices but APs were not getting an IP Address. Unplugging AP from PoE switch and plugging back in caused AP to no longer gain power from switch. Verified that APs can get power when using PoE injector but still not getting IP, so rebooted switch. After rebooting, the Cisco switch is stuck at POST with blinking green light. Rebooted multiple times and got same result. Will need to replace switch tomorrow.

TimeEntry.Title: Catalyst problem 3750
TimeEntry.Notes: We visited the site due to an internet outage and determined the issue was likely caused by a faulty fan in the switch. After checking the firewall, testing the telephone line via laptop, accessing Sophos, and reviewing the Meraki firewall, we confirmed the access points weren’t powering on—ultimately identifying the switch as the source of the problem.

TimeEntry.Title: Catalyst problem 3750
TimeEntry.Notes: After searching through all the Cisco 3750X switches in the lab—including the PoE models—I found two that matched the client's hardware. Out of those two, only one is fully functional. The other unit, marked with an X, is not working properly. I also reviewed some 3750-V2 models but confirmed that the 3750X series is the best fit for our needs. Using a console cable, I verified that the hardware status of the working unit is good and all components are operating normally.

TimeEntry.Title: Catalyst problem 3750
TimeEntry.Notes: Cleared the switch configuration and reset it to a clean, factory-ready state. The device is now fully prepared and ready for deployment at the client’s location.

TimeEntry.Title: Catalyst problem 3750
TimeEntry.Notes:
A site visit was completed to replace the existing network switch that had been causing connectivity issues. The old switch was removed and inspected to determine whether its previous configuration could be recovered; however, no usable configuration data was found.

A replacement Cisco 3750X switch was brought to the location. The team connected to the device and performed a full configuration based on the client’s current network layout and operational requirements. All necessary VLANs, trunk/access ports, and interface settings were reapplied to match the environment.

Once the configuration was completed, the new switch was installed and integrated into the network. Network connectivity was restored successfully. Verification was performed throughout the office to ensure all users regained connectivity, and all endpoints were functioning properly.

Firewall operations were also checked and confirmed to be working as expected. All switch ports required for the client’s setup were configured, tested, and validated. The system is now stable, and all network services are operating normally.

TimeEntry.Title: Switch Replacement & Configuration
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Switch Replacement, Configuration & Backup

Worked with Sunny and Sai to replace and configure the faulty network switch. Installed the new switch, applied full configuration, enabled required services, and verified functionality. After completing all settings, a full configuration backup was taken and stored for future recovery.

Key actions completed:

  • Replaced faulty switch with new hardware

  • Set hostname and configured secure enable secret

  • Generated RSA keys and enabled SSH for remote management

  • Created admin user and configured VTY lines for secure login

  • Assigned management IP and default gateway

  • Configured uplink port to router

  • Configured AP ports (trunk mode)

  • Configured phone ports with voice/data VLAN and enabled PoE

  • Verified AP and phone connectivity

  • Saved configuration to memory

  • Performed full backup of the running configuration after final setup

Network is fully operational and configuration has been safely backed up.

TimeEntry.Title: WiFi is down at the office
TimeEntry.Notes: Removed old defective Cisco switch. Lable cables from patch panel. Installed new switch. Verified that Meraki APs came back online. Verified that all desktops and phones are live. Verified printing works as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Strange Calendar Invite - :)
TimeEntry.Notes:
Reviewed the reported suspicious calendar invite received by Sarah Reed. The message appeared to impersonate Microsoft 365 Admin Center but was actually sent from:

Mason.McWherter@salib.asia
Domain: salib.asia

The invite included a potentially malicious HTML attachment and matched known phishing patterns.

Actions Performed:

  • Confirmed the sender domain as malicious.

  • Added salib.asia to the Tenant Allow/Block List → Domains & addresses with the following settings:

    • Action: Block

    • Override verdicts: Up to malware

    • Expiration: Never expire

  • This ensures all future emails or calendar invites from this domain are automatically blocked.

  • Advised the user to delete the existing calendar invite.

Status:
Domain successfully blocked,update to colleen on mail.

TimeEntry.Title: New Voicemail from 19497163329 - 19497163329
TimeEntry.Notes:
Reviewed multiple NDRs and diagnostic logs for failed email deliveries.

Confirmed error code 550 5.7.703 – Tenant Allow/Block List in Microsoft 365.

This error explicitly indicates the emails are being blocked by the sender’s Office 365 tenant (hoa-insurance.com).

Affected recipients: gloria@vintagegroupe.com
 and colleen@vintagegroupe.com
.

Emails sent from michelle@hoa-insurance.com
 to other recipients (e.g., nayeli@vintagegroupe.com
) are delivering successfully.

Confirms this is not a domain-wide block, not a recipient-side issue, and not an Exchange mail flow issue on the recipient tenant.

Failed messages never reached the recipient Exchange environment; therefore, no message trace is available on the recipient side.

Root cause identified as entries in the Tenant Allow/Block List within Michelle’s Office 365 tenant blocking the affected recipient addresses.

Resolution requires the sender’s Office 365 administrator to review and remove the blocked recipient entries from the Tenant Allow/Block List.

Once removed, allow 15–30 minutes for policy propagation before retesting delivery.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Spam
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Undeliverable / Spam Email Notifications

User reported receiving multiple “Undeliverable: We are Processing the Payment…” emails and was concerned they were sent from their mailbox. Investigation confirmed the emails were external delivery failure (bounce) notifications, not messages sent by the user or Vintage Groupe.

Analysis of message details (including INKY metadata) shows the emails originated from an external domain (rstcpa.com), with SPF, DKIM, and DMARC passing for that sender. No outbound messages were found in Microsoft Exchange Message Trace, confirming the emails did not originate from the tenant.

INKY correctly flagged the messages due to payment-related content and external origin. User was advised that their account was not compromised, no action is required, and the messages can be safely ignored or deleted. Safe Sender allow-listing was not recommended to avoid weakening email security.

TimeEntry.Title: Automatic reply: Auto Payment Set Up
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Investigated reported fraudulent email activity from ACH@vintagegroupre.com.

  • Confirmed the address is a shared mailbox, not a distribution list.

  • Verified mailbox permissions:

    • Full Access: jaime@vintagegroupre.com

    • No Send As or Send on Behalf permissions assigned to any users.

  • Message trace confirmed emails were automatic replies generated by Microsoft Exchange Online, triggered when external senders emailed the ACH mailbox.

  • No user login activity, no credential compromise, and no security breach identified.

  • Identified two external recipients who received the automatic replies.

  • Presented findings to the client and recommended disabling automatic replies and restricting external senders.

  • Client requested complete deletion of the ACH mailbox.

  • Follow-up sent to client requesting confirmation on whether a mailbox backup is required prior to permanent deletion.

  • Awaiting client confirmation before proceeding with deletion.

TimeEntry.Title: FW: 29755 Courage Court: DRA - Plans to be placed in the mail.
TimeEntry.Notes: Performed comprehensive troubleshooting on velta@vintagegroupre.com internal domain email delivery failures despite internal routing. Verified mailbox quota not exceeded, reviewed all Mail flow rules showing no matching blocks/DLP policies affecting vintagegroupre.com domain traffic, adjusted Default inbound Anti-spam policy Connection filter confirming sender IPs not listed in blocks, checked Quarantine (Bulk/Spam tabs) finding no held messages.  Executed fresh Message Trace showing persistent "Filtered"/"Failed" status on internal messages with no specific SMTP error codes identifying root cause. All standard Exchange Online mail flow components verified normal but delivery issue persists unresolved.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: FW: Your archive mailbox is almost full. [ thread::DJb_HWWqIZGR2bAu7ybYHAc:: ]
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the email instructions, we accessed the Microsoft 365 portal, verified the details, and shared the below update with Colleen.


Hi Colleen, 

We have checked and noticed that Sarah’s archive mailbox is approaching its storage limit, with 90.15 GB used out of the available 100 GB. As a result, Sarah has received a warning indicating that the archive storage is nearing capacity.

Currently, with the Office 365 E3 license, the archive mailbox has a maximum storage limit of 100 GB. To continue archiving emails beyond this limit, we recommend upgrading to the Office 365 E5 plan. This upgrade includes auto-expanding archiving, which allows the archive mailbox to automatically grow beyond 100 GB and helps avoid any disruption to the archiving process.

Benefits of upgrading to Office 365 E5 include:

  • Auto-expanding archiving, enabling the archive mailbox to grow without manual intervention.
  • Enhanced security features and advanced compliance tools included with the E5 plan.

Alternatively, if you prefer, you can manage the archive storage manually by deleting unnecessary items or moving older emails to free up space.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please let us know how you would like to proceed, and feel free to reach out if you have any questions

TimeEntry.Title: This is what I get
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the issue reported by the user where Renee was unable to open the Microsoft To Do list despite successfully receiving emails from the distribution list. Identified that the error message she encountered was related to Microsoft attempting to create a passkey during account setup, which is a device/browser-specific prompt and not related to the DL or the To Do link itself. Advised that Renee does not need a passkey to access Microsoft To Do and instructed her to bypass the passkey setup by selecting Cancel and choosing Use password instead or Skip. Also recommended signing in via https://account.microsoft.com/ using Chrome or a computer to complete the Microsoft account setup for her Gmail address without creating a passkey. Explained that once she signs in successfully, she should be able to open the shared Microsoft To Do list without issues.

TimeEntry.Title: SharePoint- Check Request Form
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed SharePoint/Microsoft Forms routing for the Check Request form. Identified that email notifications are not handled by SharePoint or Forms directly but through a Power Automate flow owned by the ThinkAI Admin service account. Verified that current permissions do not allow modification of notification recipients. Informed user that only the ThinkAI Admin account can update the routing. Contacted Manisha Verma regarding access to the ThinkAI Admin account; she confirmed she will review but cannot address immediately and expects an update by Monday/Tuesday. Awaiting her confirmation to proceed.

Ticket Summary – 02/16/2026 (President’s Day)

As assigned, due to the holiday (President’s Day – 02/16/2026), the office had a recorded holiday message in place.

Downloaded the recorded message initially and then re-downloaded it again after an hour to ensure the latest version was captured. Merged the recordings and converted the final file to meet 3CX requirements (mono/single channel, 8kHz compression format).

Uploaded and applied the converted file to the main line.
Tested inbound call flow — confirmed the message is playing correctly and functioning as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Vintage Group 3cx
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a request from Colleen Rozen regarding Paloma being out sick. As per the request, the following actions were taken to ensure that all of Paloma's calls are forwarded to Cici Brown (Extension 328):

  1. Configured Call Forwarding for Unanswered and Busy Calls:
    • External calls and internal calls to Paloma (Extension 337) will now be forwarded to Cici Brown (Extension 328).
    • The No Answer Timeout is set to 1 second, meaning if Paloma doesn't answer, the call will immediately forward to Cici Brown.
    • Calls made to Paloma when she is busy or not registered will also be forwarded to Cici Brown.
  2. No Voicemail Setup:
    • Voicemail forwarding is disabled, ensuring that all calls are directly forwarded to Cici Brown instead of going to voicemail.
  3. Email Confirmation to Colleen:
    • After completing the configuration, I sent an email to Colleen Rozen confirming that the call forwarding for Paloma has been successfully set up and all calls will now go to Cici Brown while Paloma is out sick.

Current Status:
The call forwarding has been successfully configured for Paloma. All calls will now be forwarded to Cici Brown (Extension 328). The email has been sent to Colleen Rozen confirming the setup.

TimeEntry.Title: Vintage Group - Bency Molina
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Investigated issue with Bency Molina’s line where incoming calls were intermittently routing incorrectly (reported going to Lia Dalhover, while direct app calls were reaching Bency correctly).

  • Verified user configuration and call routing settings
  • Reconfigured user settings; issue persisted
  • Removed DID number from the portal and reconfigured call routing
  • Issue still remained after initial changes
  • Fully removed the DID from the portal and reattached it to the SIP account
  • Reassigned the number to the user and performed test calls

After reconfiguration:

  • Calls successfully routed to the correct user
  • Confirmed testing from Cici’s phone — issue resolved
  • User is now receiving calls as expected

Status: Resolved ?

TimeEntry.Title: FW: Important Security Notice: Fraudulent Spoofing Attempts
TimeEntry.Notes: Got an email from colleen regarding blocking a suspicious email sender. Logged into the GoDaddy Admin portal and created a block policy for the phishing email by blocking the entire suspicious sender domain. After implementing the block, sent a confirmation email to the user. The issue was resolved successfully, and the ticket was closed.
